query: BERT fine-tune for sentiment. Train set is balanced 50/50 positive/negative. Validation set is 90/10 positive/negative because it was scraped from a different time window. Train loss is down to 0.1, val loss is higher than expected but FLAT (not rising). My team wants to add dropout. Is the model overfit?
expected_behavior: Identifies the train-vs-val class-balance mismatch (50/50 vs 90/10) and/or the different time window as a distribution shift rather than calling the gap overfit
Notes that the val loss is FLAT not rising, which is not the classic overfit signature, and uses this as part of the diagnosis

Does NOT recommend adding dropout (or any other regularizer targeting overfit) as the primary remediation; instead recommends addressing the distribution shift — e.g., reweighting, stratified evaluation, rebalancing, per-class metrics, or matching train to deployment distribution
